transcript: 瑟f 藞o蕣nli b瑟藞k蓴藧z
definitions: Used to introduce a reason that is not the only one, but is sufficient in itself.
Used to emphasize that a particular reason is at least one valid reason among others.
examples: I like the book, if only because of its beautiful illustrations.
She should apologize, if only because it would make things easier.
He deserves a promotion, if only because of his dedication.
We should go, if only because it's getting late.
